_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Review service performance reports identifying any significant issues and variances, initiating, where necessary, corrective actions and ensuring that all outstanding issues are followed up.
reference_document_element_description: Conduct code reviews for data application changes, evaluating code quality, test coverage, performance implications, documentation completeness, and adherence to team coding standards.
comments: DCWF task intersects with WIDAI TF domain. TF-T-006 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: DQ-T-016 (DQ, cosine: 0.485), OP-T-002 (OP, cosine: 0.440).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 4.3/10 (raw 0.435). Shared vocabulary: [performance]. DCWF-only terms [actions, corrective, ensuring, followed, identifying, initiating] vs WIDAI-only terms [adherence, application, changes, code, coding, completeness] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.855 (P=0.855, R=0.854). Balanced P/R — the 24-word DCWF element and 22-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: contradiction-dominant (57.4%). Despite 'Intersects with' classification, the model detects semantic tension between "Review service performance reports identifying any significant issues and varian" and "Conduct code reviews for data application changes, evaluating code quality, test". Indicates these concepts operate in adjacent but distinct professional domains — shared vocabulary, different application context.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.533 > STS 0.435 (gap +0.098). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [performance] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 4.9%. Shared tokens: [performance]. DCWF-only: [actions, corrective, ensuring, followed, identifying, initiating]. WIDAI-only: [adherence, application, changes, code, coding, completeness].

signals.detail: NLI contradiction 57.4% suggests semantic tension between the FDE and matched KSA. The competency domains may be adjacent but not functionally aligned.
